💬 General Chat / development / nRF52 iphone BLE lockup thread
recrof 2025-11-10 5:13 p.m.
@Random Citizen @che aporeps 🌮 our base of operations
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-10 5:14 p.m.
Nice
5:14 p.m.
@Random Citizen good stuff on the iphone BLE lockup branch, i can see you've been hard at work
recrof 2025-11-10 5:16 p.m.
also @Liam Cottle wanted to delve into this stuff
5:16 p.m.
dream team!
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-10 5:21 p.m.
Ok I'll fill you in on where I'm at. I've spent the weekend hacking up repeater to SerialBLEInterface so I could get MESH_PACKET_LOGGING from repeater via BLE (it's working but it needs a bit of shine before I show it to public). Along the way while debugging I did notice that we can attempt to write more than the bleuart buffer but that doesn't cause any crashes, bleuart is quite forgiving and it will split writes along MTU lines, and silently drop whatever didn't fit into the FIFO. So that's good. (edited)
5:22 p.m.
Anyway, what I did notice is that we don't seem to actually check if the BLE connection is active before we check our send_queue and then attempt to do bleuart.write(), and I think that's what happens with the iPhone crashes.
5:23 p.m.
iPhone maybe closes connection very fast and if we still have things in the send_queue we just blindly hit bleuart.write() and if there's no active handle then you get lockup.
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-10 5:39 p.m.
The other thing might be to add a small delay in onDisconnect before we call startAdv again? Maybe even call disable() and then enable() to ensure it's all truly clean?
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-10 7:45 p.m.
Of course I might just be full of shit, I have covid and i definitely feel full of shit.
che aporeps 🌮
@Random Citizen good stuff on the iphone BLE lockup branch, i can see you've been hard at work
Random Citizen 2025-11-11 12:13 a.m.
My eyes are bleeding
12:14 a.m.
I noticed that if disconnect reason is 19 (we initiated) everything's good
12:15 a.m.
But if it's 8 (timeout) and i open the microwave, starts syncing again AND close again, it locks up
12:16 a.m.
But that's far i got yesterday, i rolled back to dev interface and tried something more simple (clear handles and make a clean disconnect on reason 8) (edited)
12:16 a.m.
Not tested it yet, maybe this afternoon i'll have time
che aporeps 🌮
The other thing might be to add a small delay in onDisconnect before we call startAdv again? Maybe even call disable() and then enable() to ensure it's all truly clean?
Random Citizen 2025-11-11 12:21 a.m.
According to nordic docs we shouldn't call startadv because its heavy, irritates the softdevice, let bluefruit handle it. Maybe that's there because someone noticed strange behaviors and tried to force
Random Citizen 2025-11-11 12:37 a.m.
Hmm, another thing: Yesterdays constant testing made my android app connect twice again ( i see "heard 2 repeats" but only 1 repeater) I can only see 1 app, nothing strange but sonething is running twice in the background
Random Citizen
Hmm, another thing: Yesterdays constant testing made my android app connect twice again ( i see "heard 2 repeats" but only 1 repeater) I can only see 1 app, nothing strange but sonething is running twice in the background
recrof 2025-11-11 1:11 a.m.
yeah, the bluetooth hooks get doubled, so every action is triggered 2x.
1:12 a.m.
would be awesome to have this reliably replicable for Liam, so he can figure out why it happens (edited)
Random Citizen 2025-11-11 1:26 a.m.
Well, you need my "Cursor! That's too much!" codebase and a microwave oven
1:32 a.m.
@che aporeps 🌮 i see you are poking around the same thing i did, i have a tester guy he's got my latest code, if it freezes again i'll merge your idea too and make him a uf2 Also @Liam Cottle do you need device info and battery service through BLE? And need leading zeros on pins? Those are two sidetracks I discovered in the last few days, that can be added.

1:35 a.m.

@che aporeps 🌮 i see you are poking around the same thing i did, i have a tester guy he's got my latest code, if it freezes again i'll merge your idea too and make him a uf2 Also @Liam Cottle do you need device info and battery service through BLE? Liam Cottle 2025-11-11 2:38 a.m.
Nah, I don't use the info/battery characteristics at all. I use the companion firmware commands to fetch that
1
2:39 a.m.
regarding ble pins, I added firmware validation a while back the restricts the allowed pins from 100,000-999,999. to avoid issues with pins not being 6 characters when parsed as an int etc
1
Random Citizen 2025-11-11 3:35 a.m.
Also a sidenote: we can prevent bluefruit using the ble led if we want to control it ourselves
recrof 2025-11-11 3:47 a.m.
yeah, we probably should change that, so blue led is not lit all the time
Liam Cottle 2025-11-11 3:56 a.m.

3:57 a.m.
"blue light is solid but app not connected" is a good way for users to tell us the state
1
3:58 a.m.

Liam Cottle
would be interesting to measure the power consumption with led on/off. I doubt it would be massive savings
recrof 2025-11-11 4:33 a.m.

true, guess that doesn't affect me since my cases have the rak led on the bottom, which means it's covered unless you pick it up

recrof 2025-11-11 4:30 p.m.
just realized that MT must have something fundamentally different in their adafruit bluetooth stack/LittleFS, as our pairing databases aren't compatible. normally, one would assume that the pairing data is the same, so devices that are paired will get recongized and let in without pin, but that is not the case
4:30 p.m.
do I miss something here?
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-11 10:39 p.m.
i didn't realise that the pairing doesn't work between MT and MC, that's surprising.
10:39 p.m.
do they set up with different security maybe?

Liam Cottle 2025-11-12 12:53 a.m.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-12 1:01 a.m.

1:03 a.m.
@Liam Cottle Basically if you connect to the device, and then put your phone in the microwave and shut the door, you'll see the light start blinking when it starts advertising, that's your cue to open the door and let the light go solid again, then close the door straight away. Do that a few times and you might get it to crash on android... I'm not sure but it maybe having a stacked contacts list makes it more likely to happen, because it's busy sending the contacts to the phone.
1:04 a.m.
The microwave simulates the bluefruit 0x8 timeout disconnect... I think it's probably an issue on android as well, but iPhone sets the supervisor timeour to 750ms, whereas android (mine anyway) sets it to 2000ms
1:06 a.m.
I think it's reconnecting too quickly before the softdevice has settled after the 0x8 disconnect can cause the nrf52 to crash? Maybe us manually controlling the advertising restart is to partly to blame?
che aporeps 🌮
The microwave simulates the bluefruit 0x8 timeout disconnect... I think it's probably an issue on android as well, but iPhone sets the supervisor timeour to 750ms, whereas android (mine anyway) sets it to 2000ms
Liam Cottle 2025-11-12 1:07 a.m.
I guess this is why it's more common on iOS than android
1

Liam Cottle 2025-11-12 2:46 a.m.
one thing to note that might be interesting, is the app manually fetches each channel, and doesn't do it if the app knows it's disconnected from firmware. however, the contacts are pushed one after the other by the firmware

hmm, some fresh info, thanks!
Liam Cottle 2025-11-12 2:47 a.m.

Liam Cottle
switch to true, and remove instance->startAdv in onDisconnect? void SerialBLEInterface::onDisconnect(uint16_t connection_handle, uint8_t reason) { BLE_DEBUG_PRINTLN("SerialBLEInterface: disconnected reason=%d", reason); if(instance){ instance->_isDeviceConnected = false; instance->startAdv(); } } (edited)

che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-12 5:23 a.m.
https://github.com/oltaco/MeshCore/tree/ble-fixattempt
GitHub - oltaco/MeshCore at ble-fixattempt
A new lightweight, hybrid routing mesh protocol for packet radios - GitHub - oltaco/MeshCore at ble-fixattempt
5:24 a.m.
firmware cooking now... the two changes are basically early return from checkRecvFrame and a 10 second delay after disconnect before we start advertising again
recrof 2025-11-12 5:25 a.m.
isn't 10 too long?
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-12 5:25 a.m.
probably, but i wanted to be sure
recrof 2025-11-12 5:25 a.m.
and what happens between disconnect/reconnect that causes the lockups (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-12 5:26 a.m.
I think it's not the disconnect, it's the reconnect afterwards... something is not ready?
5:27 a.m.
Orrrr...... it wasn't the delay that fixed it and it's just returning straight away from checkRecvFrame if we aren't connected?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-12 5:28 a.m.
could remove the delay and see if it breaks again
5:28 a.m.
if not, early return helped

Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:47 p.m.
@Random Citizen just tried out the btfixv3 branch SUPER FAST
10:47 p.m.
I've pushed an update to the web app, and it will be in next mobile app update too, to use sqlite database transactions for the contacts and channels upsert. The contacts pretty much sync instantly now
3
3
10:47 p.m.
channels still slow as before, but no longer sits at the end 40/40 waiting for a bit
10:48 p.m.
I think there's some under the hood delays with flutter blue plus, and mutexes and just dart/platform ipc overhead for writes
10:48 p.m.
was a couple hundred milliseconds or so between writes
10:48 p.m.
can't seem to get that any faster
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 10:50 p.m.
can't wait
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:51 p.m.
the alternative is to add a new bulk pushed based channel fetch to firmware, like the contacts sync
10:51 p.m.
currently, you have to fetch each channel index one by one
10:51 p.m.
this speed increase is gonna blow peoples minds
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 10:52 p.m.
probably would be better, or at least something to stop fetching empty channels
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:52 p.m.
https://tenor.com/3p2e.gif
2
che aporeps 🌮
probably would be better, or at least something to stop fetching empty channels
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:52 p.m.
yeah, issue with this, is channels can be in higher slots, even if lower slots deleted
10:52 p.m.
actually had this chat with other admins the other day
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 10:52 p.m.
ah gotcha
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:53 p.m.
either you have to fetch all slots, or you have to rearrange all existing channels anytime you delete one
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 10:53 p.m.
makes sense, most things are how they are for one reason or another
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:53 p.m.
alternative is new sync mechanism in firmware, behind a protocol version bump (edited)
10:54 p.m.
I opted for fetching all, to avoid any issues with rearranging
10:54 p.m.
plus can't force other clients to do it
10:54 p.m.
this was an issue I ran into with MT
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 10:54 p.m.
yeah definitely
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:55 p.m.
their Android/iOS clients would rearrange the channels
10:55 p.m.
so when I deleted/added channels in my own client, official clients didn't show newer channels...
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 10:56 p.m.
yeah these are all things i have thought about for a chunked contacts system so we don't have one giant file which we have to write in entirety every time a single contact changes
1
10:58 p.m.
you want a header in each chunk that specifies any deleted records that can be overwritten, so you can avoid shuffling contacts around in chunks and causing cascading writes
10:58 p.m.
otherwise if you delete a contact from the first chunk you have to shuffle all the contacts back and that means writing every chunk...
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 10:59 p.m.
yeah, littlefs doesn't like when you want to write stuff......
10:59 p.m.
little freezing shit
10:59 p.m.
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 10:59 p.m.
littlefs doesn't like it when you store it's metadata pairs in the same erase page either
1
11:00 p.m.
i wanted to increase block size to 4k, but each metadata block is then 4k, and you run out of that 100k very quickly
11:01 p.m.
4k blocks = 16kb gone to an empty filesystem without any files at all
1
11:03 p.m.
there may be a middle ground that could work... it would be great to be able to write contacts as chunks, and use atomic writes so that if we lose power while writing it's just that chunk that gets lost and not the entire filesystem
Liam Cottle 2025-11-13 11:04 p.m.
yeah, I'm all for more robust fs
11:04 p.m.
I'm scared everytime I give someone a new device
11:04 p.m.
that they're gonna hit me up "uhhh, why is it frozen"
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-13 11:04 p.m.
yeah i know what ya mean
11:04 p.m.
the external flash seems a lot more forgiving at least
11:06 p.m.
anyway we should stop derailing the ble thread
Random Citizen 2025-11-14 1:16 a.m.
Anyone looked into littlefs v2 or the experimental v3(if out?) Or those are not so "little" anymore and we can't use it?
Liam Cottle
I've pushed an update to the web app, and it will be in next mobile app update too, to use sqlite database transactions for the contacts and channels upsert. The contacts pretty much sync instantly now
Random Citizen 2025-11-14 1:16 a.m.
Finally the app catches up
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-14 1:16 a.m.
Yes, like double the overhead
1:17 a.m.
So not really doable
1
1:20 a.m.
yeah, here's the info https://github.com/littlefs-project/littlefs/issues/95#issuecomment-1593479575
1:21 a.m.
could do it for external flash only i guess but it adds more complexity and doesn't help the majority of users
1:22 a.m.
i think a mid range like 2kb blocks and chunked contacts file might do the trick though
1:24 a.m.
the flash has 4k erase but only 2k write, but then adafruit littlefs cooks it with 128 byte blocks (they didn't have much choice for block size, because they chose tiny 28kb userdata area)
Downet 2025-11-14 5:33 a.m.

3:23 a.m.

Random Citizen
I refuse to believe it "just needs some delay" but i've read a lot of documentation lately so my opinion could be biased (everybody knows that everything is rainbows and ponies in documentations) (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-15 4:05 a.m.

4:08 a.m.
Obviously MT doesn't have any delay but also how much data actually gets sent between the phone and device at connect?
4:08 a.m.
Or at all for that matter
4:09 a.m.
Maybe they delay any data sending until the connection has been up for a while? No delay on advertising but delay on data sending after new connection comes up
4:09 a.m.
All just thoughts
Random Citizen 2025-11-15 4:24 a.m.
Yes, thats exactly what I asked from Liam in DM.
4:25 a.m.
MT ios app waits for full connection before sending and also tears down everything on disconnect. Even on timeout specifically
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-15 4:26 a.m.
Makes sense
4:26 a.m.
Still a delay, it just happens elsewhere
Random Citizen 2025-11-15 4:45 a.m.

recrof 2025-11-16 3:45 a.m.

3:46 a.m.
and he was able to reproduce it on iphone 8 even.. but it's much harder (edited)
3:48 a.m.
@Liam Cottle ip 15 will work just fine, you don't need pro/max
3:49 a.m.
i'm pretty sure even iphone SE 2022 is able to get the bug consistently
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 3:50 a.m.
might have found it
3:50 a.m.
probably buffer overflow
3:50 a.m.
size_t len = _serial->checkRecvFrame(cmd_frame);
3:51 a.m.
uint8_t cmd_frame[MAX_FRAME_SIZE + 1];
3:51 a.m.
cmd_frame is 173 in size
3:52 a.m.
if anything happened to send data over that size, it would overflow
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 3:52 a.m.
hmm isn't size_t alright with uint8_t?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 3:52 a.m.
I don't think the app does this at all, but maybe there could be multiple packets that got sent as one
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 3:53 a.m.
ble max mtu is 250 though, and then you take off 3 for the gatt header so it's really 247...
Liam Cottle
I don't think the app does this at all, but maybe there could be multiple packets that got sent as one
recrof 2025-11-16 3:53 a.m.
maybe it's the BT stack, not the app?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 3:53 a.m.
tbh, won't know until I get hardware
3:54 a.m.
I missed that we were setting BLEDevice::setMTU(MAX_FRAME_SIZE);
3:54 a.m.
still a strange number to use for mtu
recrof
@Liam Cottle ip 15 will work just fine, you don't need pro/max
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 3:54 a.m.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 3:55 a.m.
we are?? i thought it was set to 250 by this line Bluefruit.configPrphConn(250, BLE_GAP_EVENT_LENGTH_MIN, 16, 16); // increase MTU
3:55 a.m.
and that's the max supported by nrf52840
che aporeps 🌮
we are?? i thought it was set to 250 by this line Bluefruit.configPrphConn(250, BLE_GAP_EVENT_LENGTH_MIN, 16, 16); // increase MTU
Random Citizen 2025-11-16 3:55 a.m.
You don't even need to set it to 250 there. BANDWIDTH_MAX sets it to 247 (the max)
3:56 a.m.
I scrubbed that line
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 3:56 a.m.
I'm just making this more confusing..

3:56 a.m.

3:56 a.m.
...

Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:00 a.m.
I could spam the 0x08 disconnect with the no antenna method (edited)
recrof 2025-11-16 4:01 a.m.
important note: it doesn't happen right after disconnect, but after you reconnect
4:01 a.m.
the hypotesis is: the disconnect corrupts something and then the reconect causes the lockup (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:02 a.m.
yep, that ble len=2 packet and bam
4:04 a.m.
all of the logs that people sent in on the github issue have the same thing, an 0x08 disconnect (usually 2 or more in reasonably quick succession) and then the log ends right after a len=2
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:04 a.m.
that two byte packet will be the device query
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:04 a.m.
yep
4:04 a.m.
that's why i went looking at the contacts iterator
4:05 a.m.
oh you think maybe it's the device query hitting the fs size check even?
4:06 a.m.
hmm.. does this bug affect any devices with external flash?
4:07 a.m.
fs free space check is kinda costly, causes a bunch of filesystem reads while it follows the skip list to count blocks in use.
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:07 a.m.
I was meaning this one
4:07 a.m.
this is the first query app makes
4:07 a.m.
that's the 2 byte one
4:10 a.m.
it does read the battery level no too long after, and that request includes an fs check for size
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:10 a.m.
yeah so i would say it crashes on the first received packet after reconnect from 0x08 disconnect (more likely if it's had more than one 0x08 disconnect)
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:10 a.m.
woud expect other logs to show up before then, but maybe possible that serial logs aren't printed out fast enough
4:10 a.m.
similar to how I showed the other day, had to spam logs to get it to show
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:10 a.m.
couple logs here https://pastebin.com/PxT4UADG https://pastebin.com/AmZTEgj1

Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:10 a.m.
since little fs would lock up before logs show
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:11 a.m.
it may also not be littlefs but actually the softdevice.. since the softdevice is busy with ble connect and disconnect stuff, and then we ask it to do a stack of fs reads at the same time?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:12 a.m.
yeah getting into the weeds for sure
4:12 a.m.
it's all going to be assumptions until I get the hw (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:12 a.m.
that's why i wondered if the crash affects devices with external flash, since they dont use the softdevice to read fs used blocks
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:13 a.m.
rak4631 was said to be affected, and that's using extrafs?
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:13 a.m.
extrafs is still not external flash, it's just another area of onchip flash
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:13 a.m.
which ones do support external
4:14 a.m.
QSPIFLASH=1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:14 a.m.
wio L1 / techo /
4:14 a.m.
yeah... thinknode m1
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:14 a.m.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:15 a.m.
ooh, wio_wm1110 didn't know that!
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 4:15 a.m.

recrof 2025-11-16 4:15 a.m.
@che aporeps 🌮 it happens to t-echo as well. but doesn't matter, because bluefruit still uses internal flash to store BLE metadata

4:15 a.m.
my bet is on softdevice
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:16 a.m.
mmm that's true... but it's only reading those cccd files and not writing them? maybe it does trip over though
4:17 a.m.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 4:17 a.m.

4:19 a.m.
i was looking on nordic devzone at a post from someone whose nrf52833 device is broken on ios26, causes it to reconnect endlessly. ios26 apparently sends some sort of ble update connection parameters packet every 120ms and the device crashes
4:21 a.m.
i realise it's not the same issue but it does point to ios26 being unfriendly https://devzone.nordicsemi.com/f/nordic-q-a/124994/ios-26-bluetooth-disconnect-issues

1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 9:25 p.m.
tbh bluefruit/bleuart is actually a lot more forgiving than i realised. if you feed it frames that are above MTU it will handle that by just splitting it across packets no probs, up until a point where the fifo gets full and it silently drops bits and pieces
9:25 p.m.
calling write when not connected doesn't seem to worry it
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 10:00 p.m.
OOOOOOH
10:00 p.m.
I enabled CFG_DEBUG=3 and wasn't able to get it to trigger anymore, probably because it's such a timing sensitive bug
10:00 p.m.
but, after about 10 mins of connecting/disconnecting, the rak board rebooted itself
10:01 p.m.
I saw something about CFG_DEBUG causing the softdevice to reset the board for failing APP_ERROR_CHECK (edited)
mcarper 2025-11-16 10:03 p.m.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heisenbug
Heisenbug
In computer programming jargon, a heisenbug is a software bug that seems to disappear or alter its behavior when one attempts to study it. The term is a pun on the name of Werner Heisenberg, the physicist who first introduced the uncertainty principle, and it is a reference to the observer effect, which states that the act of observing a system ...
Liam Cottle
I saw something about CFG_DEBUG causing the softdevice to reset the board for failing APP_ERROR_CHECK (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 10:19 p.m.
Interesting
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 10:47 p.m.
yeah that reboot with cfg_debug seems reliable
10:47 p.m.
instead of hanging, it just reboots
10:48 p.m.
so it's either some internal assert, app_error_check or a stack overflow
Liam Cottle
so it's either some internal assert, app_error_check or a stack overflow
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 11:08 p.m.
so this happens with CFG_DEBUG=3 and just leaving the device alone for a few minutes and not triggering BLE timeouts?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-16 11:14 p.m.
  • board will lock up if CFG_DEBUG build flag is missing
  • board will auto reboot if CFG_DEBUG=3 build flag is set
11:14 p.m.
still needs the timeout trigger stuff
11:14 p.m.
I've just added hooks for the app_error_fault_handler and assert_nrf_callback functions
11:14 p.m.
trying to get it to trigger the issue with those set
11:15 p.m.
it's definitely much harder to trigger with cfg_debug=3 set, and I think it's because it spits out so many logs, the timing issue is harder to hit
2
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-16 11:20 p.m.

recrof 2025-11-17 9:05 a.m.
guys here swear that this fix works for freezes: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/872#issuecomment-3541976736
Fix/serialble overflow guard by spacepc-de · Pull Request #872 · ...
Fixes a potential buffer overflow when reading from the Bluefruit BLE UART on nRF52 by clamping reads to MAX_FRAME_SIZE and draining any surplus bytes. No protocol or API changes; normal behavior ...
1
1
Random Citizen 2025-11-17 9:23 a.m.
are their microwave door squeaking?
Downet 2025-11-17 9:27 a.m.

1
recrof
guys here swear that this fix works for freezes: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/872#issuecomment-3541976736
Random Citizen 2025-11-17 9:29 a.m.
hmm, it seems we have MTU 250 set and BaseSerialInterface.h sets MAX_FRAME_SIZE = 172
9:30 a.m.
so it would be possible for an iOS to send bigger frame that stuck in softdevice?
9:32 a.m.
and it only triggered overflow when something unexpected happens and multiple frames stuck together so they are bigger than 172?
9:32 a.m.
and they are just sitting there in the softdevice queue but we don't touch it because: int len = bleuart.available(); if (len > 0 && len <= MAX_FRAME_SIZE) { bleuart.readBytes(dest, len); return len; } (edited)
Downet
Device down again, no bt connection as well

AlexJDow 2025-11-17 12:47 p.m.
Howdy, @MrAlders0n suggested I add my experience here. Two devices: T1000-e and Heltec T114. FW: 1.10 (but also happened on 1.9) iPhone: 16pro Behavior/Observations:
  • Companions will become unavailable to Bluetooth (iphone) and physically unresponsive, at least once a day
  • Heltec: Reboot button seems to not work, I end up opening/closing the battery circuit to bring it back to life
  • T1000-e: Button does not respond regardless of how long I hold or patterns. I have to plug it into USB charger and it resets and becomes available.
  • NEW: I left both companions away from iphone for the weekend and they were both available this morning. I question @MrAlders0n 's suggest this freezing is iphone/BT related, but onboard with the hypothesis now.
Hope that helps, tag me if you need me to perform any further tests, share data.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-18 8:19 a.m.

8:20 a.m.
But that post makes me wonder about our use of "this"
8:22 a.m.
@Random Citizen specifically this line makes me wonder. I think what was happening was that the bluefruit framework is cleaning up connection handles on the DISCONNECT call. It's doing that by referencing "this" for the BLEClientServices/Char. Well, since I kept the variables in method scope it was causing an issue (I think that was the problem).
8:25 a.m.
Anyway I'll stop guessing from my bed, tomorrow is another day

8:25 a.m.
Night mates 🫡

yeah, this part of code is platform-dependent
12:39 p.m.

Random Citizen 2025-11-19 5:00 a.m.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-19 5:00 a.m.

Random Citizen 2025-11-19 5:02 a.m.
maybe, I only hear 1 repeat now so there are none left
Random Citizen 2025-11-19 5:28 a.m.
@recrof all 3 approaches (turbo queue, che's adv timeout, PR#872's RX drain) are married and live happily ever after (or until the first freeze) in btfixv3 3 sec grace period between disconnects and re-advertising, and telling iOS to use apple's "Peripheral Preferred Connection Parameters" too. I re-re-re-refactored it and removed any extra sh*t I crammed into it in the last days. still needs a full walkover on the code to revise everything, but that's for tonight if I have some more time
2
recrof 2025-11-19 5:29 a.m.
will distribute to testers, thanks!
Random Citizen 2025-11-19 5:31 a.m.
the only thing I saw after several rapid timeouts is the Heisenberg channel sync in @Liam Cottle 's app, but that's funny so not a regression (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-19 5:33 a.m.
It's a feature, it gives you a thrill
Random Citizen 2025-11-19 5:47 a.m.
a little sidetrack: bool RAK4631Board::startOTAUpdate(const char* id, char reply[]) { Bluefruit.configPrphBandwidth(BANDWIDTH_MAX); Bluefruit.configPrphConn(92, BLE_GAP_EVENT_LENGTH_MIN, 16, 16); we set configPrphBandwidth(BANDWIDTH_MAX) that sets the MTU to 247 but then override and set it back to 92 immediately. I think that's why we need to adjust DFU update apps to not negotiate high mtu and BLE_GAP_EVENT_LENGTH_MIN is very-very small. BANDWIDTH_MAX sets it to ~300msec not 7.5msec
1
5:52 a.m.
but maybe that's intentional for DFU
5:52 a.m.
to make it more reliable or something
Random Citizen
a little sidetrack: bool RAK4631Board::startOTAUpdate(const char* id, char reply[]) { Bluefruit.configPrphBandwidth(BANDWIDTH_MAX); Bluefruit.configPrphConn(92, BLE_GAP_EVENT_LENGTH_MIN, 16, 16); we set configPrphBandwidth(BANDWIDTH_MAX) that sets the MTU to 247 but then override and set it back to 92 immediately. I think that's why we need to adjust DFU update apps to not negotiate high mtu and BLE_GAP_EVENT_LENGTH_MIN is very-very small. BANDWIDTH_MAX sets it to ~300msec not 7.5msec
recrof 2025-11-19 5:53 a.m.
dfu apps will switch into bootloader DFU after one second, so those settings won't stick very long
1
Random Citizen 2025-11-19 5:53 a.m.
ah, right

Stéphane P 2025-11-21 9:38 a.m.
Just a thought, could we not simply make the app auto disconnect from the companion node when the app is not in use after a set timeline. I find with the faster load times for the contacts it’s really a non issue. I know this solution would make it that you would no longer get updates or notifications from the device anymore but would prevent the node from locking up completely and basically missing all incoming messages altogether. Until ble can be stable in iOS I think this could be a solution and could also be an opt in setting. Just an idea, I’m not a dev but I have been doing this manually, every time I check my stuff I disconnect from the node because it’s guaranteed to lock up otherwise. (edited)
Stéphane P
Just a thought, could we not simply make the app auto disconnect from the companion node when the app is not in use after a set timeline. I find with the faster load times for the contacts it’s really a non issue. I know this solution would make it that you would no longer get updates or notifications from the device anymore but would prevent the node from locking up completely and basically missing all incoming messages altogether. Until ble can be stable in iOS I think this could be a solution and could also be an opt in setting. Just an idea, I’m not a dev but I have been doing this manually, every time I check my stuff I disconnect from the node because it’s guaranteed to lock up otherwise. (edited)

Hey @Liam Cottle. I compiled like this, I assume this is enabling "debug flags" or is there something else? [env:Heltec_t114_companion_radio_ble] extends = Heltec_t114_with_display board_build.ldscript = boards/nrf52840_s140_v6_extrafs.ld board_upload.maximum_size = 712704 ; build with debug symbols and lower optimization build_type = debug build_flags = ${Heltec_t114_with_display.build_flags} -I examples/companion_radio/ui-new -D MAX_CONTACTS=350 -D MAX_GROUP_CHANNELS=40 -D BLE_PIN_CODE=123456 -D ENV_INCLUDE_GPS=1 ; enable the GPS page in UI -D BLE_DEBUG_LOGGING=1 -D OFFLINE_QUEUE_SIZE=256 ; -D MESH_PACKET_LOGGING=1 ; -D MESH_DEBUG=1 build_src_filter = ${Heltec_t114_with_display.build_src_filter} +<helpers/nrf52/SerialBLEInterface.cpp> +<../examples/companion_radio/*.cpp> +<../examples/companion_radio/ui-new/*.cpp> lib_deps = ${Heltec_t114_with_display.lib_deps} densaugeo/base64 @ ~1.4.0 ; optional, if you want J-Link to be used for flashing this env upload_protocol = jlink debug_tool = jlink

10:55 p.m.

2
2
Liam Cottle 2025-11-21 10:58 p.m.
yom! we getting into the summer weather here in NZ now. so bbqs gonna be on the go again
Liam Cottle 2025-11-21 11:14 p.m.
fun one. with blueuart.write and rapid disconnect/reconnect, it will 100% of the time lockup with this spam loop I have. commenting out blueuart.write and it never locks up. feels like BLE might be trying to access the buffer we pass it but later on when it's been nuked or modified by another iterator loop (edited)
Liam Cottle
fun one. with blueuart.write and rapid disconnect/reconnect, it will 100% of the time lockup with this spam loop I have. commenting out blueuart.write and it never locks up. feels like BLE might be trying to access the buffer we pass it but later on when it's been nuked or modified by another iterator loop (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-21 11:19 p.m.
This was my thought as well and doing things in callbacks made it worse. Feels like a race condition with using the handle as it's in the process of getting torn down by disconnect reason=8 but no matter the guards it still occurs. Backing off with the delay seemed to help as well
1
Liam Cottle 2025-11-21 11:21 p.m.
yeah, even ignoring out frame queue entirely, and just spam sending this kills it: uint8_t garbage[100]; bleuart.write(garbage, sizeof(garbage));
1
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 2:19 a.m.
Still can't comprehend why it's only with iOS
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 2:31 a.m.
going down the rabbit hole, have added logging into bluefruit and where the softdevice calls happen...
2:31 a.m.
seems to crash when releasing a semaphore for hvn packets
2:32 a.m.
soft device returned an error code saying ble wasn't enabled, and then goes to release the semaphore, resulting in a crash
2:33 a.m.
sd_ble_gatts_hvx returns 0x3401 which is: #define BLE_ERROR_NOT_ENABLED (NRF_ERROR_STK_BASE_NUM+0x001) /**< @ref sd_ble_enable has not been called. */ after that, BLECharacteristic.cpp in Bluefruit lib calls conn->releaseHvnPacket(); (edited)
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 2:44 a.m.
commented out the semaphore release, and it lived for longer. was able to send/receive more packets. then ultimately crashed again in a completely new place I've never seen it crash. this time inside framework-arduinoadafruitnrf52/cores/nRF5/freertos/portable/GCC/nrf52/port.c:104
2:46 a.m.
prob because the semaphore never gets released
2:46 a.m.
the ble lib really just doesn't like us (edited)
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 4:02 a.m.
aight, this was a wild ride. I think I've found the cause... a bug in the Bluefruit lib, in the adafruit nrf52 framework
4:04 a.m.
from what i can tell, it looks like a semaphore not being released properly (edited)
4:05 a.m.
BLECharacteristic::notify acquires it with conn->getHvnPacket(), but only releases it if there's a failure after sending it to soft device. If it doesn't get an error, it doesn't release it. seems kinda strange
4:06 a.m.
4:07 a.m.
I commented out the check for failure, and release it every time. haven't seen a single crash. as soon as I add the check back in (which means it only releases on error state) it goes back to crashing
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 4:08 a.m.
Smells like a PR for adafruit
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 4:14 a.m.
4:19 a.m.
@MrAlders0n you're able to trigger this quite often eh? would be very interested if you could comment those lines out like I did in the screenshot/video, and see if this resolves it for you
4:20 a.m.
pretty much spent the last 8 hours on this... will be very happy if this is it
1
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 4:39 a.m.
When i get home in the afternoon, I'll make this change in my branch if nobody tests it until then
1
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 4:40 a.m.
would love to have it fixed and also get turbo ble into prod
4:41 a.m.
btw, all of this testing was on release v1.10.0
1
4:41 a.m.
no using any of the external branches that have been passed around
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 4:41 a.m.
Sounds good
Liam Cottle
would love to have it fixed and also get turbo ble into prod
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 4:42 a.m.
Me too!
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 4:49 a.m.
https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/issues/443 https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/pull/444
Sporadic hangup when stopping notifies · Issue #443 · adafruit/Ad...
Describe the bug When acting as a peripheral and sending notifications at a high frequency; after central has subscribed and unsubscribed several times, the notifications will no longer be sent unt...
Fix sporadic hangup when stopping notifies - Resolves #443 by matsp...
#443
4:49 a.m.
the releasing of the semaphore here, was actually done by a pr, however they decided to only do it on failure
4:50 a.m.
wonder why
Liam Cottle
https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/issues/443 https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/pull/444
recrof 2025-11-22 4:54 a.m.
that's probably why ios app does have delay before ble re-adverts
4:54 a.m.
they did not fix the bug, so needed to go around it
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 4:56 a.m.
interesting, found the other place it releases the semaphore https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/blob/4a2d8dd5be9686b6580ed2249cae43972922572f/libraries/Bluefruit52Lib/src/BLEConnection.cpp#L419
4:56 a.m.
based on the tx complete events
4:57 a.m.
wonder if my patch is causing other issues then....
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 4:58 a.m.
tx won't complete on a timeout, maybe that's why it freezes on the edge of reception (edited)
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 4:59 a.m.
my thoughts were it was getting into a state where the semaphore queue was exhausted. whereas now, it's just instantly released, so maybe effectively no queueing
5:00 a.m.
before it was crashing when the semaphore was released on error, but now that it's released everytime, no crash
5:00 a.m.
so strange
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 5:04 a.m.
No queue = ultra turbo
5:05 a.m.
Wonder if it's THE solution, do we really need all the guards we've had in place now
1
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 5:05 a.m.
spray and pray
Random Citizen
Wonder if it's THE solution, do we really need all the guards we've had in place now
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 5:08 a.m.
from all of my testing, the guards to check if connected or notify enabled didn't even matter, because when I added logging to the bluefruit library itself, it always said it was connected, handle was valid and notifications were enabled
1
5:08 a.m.
plus it already did those checks internally
Random Citizen 2025-11-22 5:09 a.m.
Yeah, i tried to take over checks from softdevice because @recrof made me not trust it
5:09 a.m.
I'll make another branch with simpler bleinterface
Liam Cottle 2025-11-22 5:10 a.m.
I reckon just the semaphore/queue bypass, and then turbo ble writes and we will be golden
2
5:10 a.m.
if all else fails, reboot on crash
Liam Cottle

Cisien 2025-11-26 4:14 p.m.
the phone had disconnected at some point, i was distracted playing with winlink on vhf
4:15 p.m.
radiomail + ble kiss tnc on my ht
4:16 p.m.
but yeah, it went deaf over night, i rebooted (from the app) and it came back alive, then when i was playing with winlink, i heard the usb disconnected sound
mcarper 2025-11-26 4:20 p.m.
hf tx causing issues?
4:23 p.m.
What's your vhf tx power?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 4:48 p.m.
the sx1262 will go deaf when transmitting with something else off frequency nearby
4:48 p.m.
needs to do its own tx or go into standby and back to receive to fix (edited)
4:49 p.m.
easy to reproduce if you have a handheld vhf/uhf and press ptt near your node (edited)
Cisien 2025-11-26 5:17 p.m.
a transmit was received by repeaters, but didn't recover the receive
5:18 p.m.
vhf is just 8w
5:18 p.m.
(on paper)
5:18 p.m.
I flashed back to the released 1.10.0 build, had a fresh lock up when returning from the grocery store
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 7:36 p.m.
I went digging around in the adafruit github again, and I think I found something https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/issues/772 https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/pull/825 The note in the issue sounds awfully familiar, and the debug log ends exactly how our crashes end.
7:37 p.m.
What happened ? During BLE transmission of data when the mobile app goes out of range the board freezes waiting for binary semaphore to release indefinitely. How to reproduce ? Run indicate method for ble publishing in loop, remove antenna and go out of range. 11:59:19.609 -> [BLE ] BLE_GAP_EVT_DISCONNECTED : Conn Handle = 0 11:59:19.609 -> [GAP ] Disconnect Reason: CONNECTION_TIMEOUT 11:59:19.609 -> Disconnected, reason = 0x8 11:59:49.618 -> Advertising! 11:59:49.618 -> [BLE ] BLE_GAP_EVT_ADV_SET_TERMINATED : Conn Handle = 65535
7:37 p.m.
What do you think @Liam Cottle ?
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 8:39 p.m.
Ok I think I understand, it's the same issue just with the confirm packets rather than notify packets like we use.
8:43 p.m.
What if instead of just giving back the semaphore straight away, we were to give it back here? https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/blob/4a2d8dd5be9686b6580ed2249cae43972922572f/libraries/Bluefruit52Lib/src/BLEConnection.cpp#L307
8:46 p.m.
Technically since notify events don't expect a response our spray and pray approach is probably fine, unless we spray too many at the softdevice without it having a chance to spray them on... and then it will maybe either start returning errors or possibly worse
che aporeps 🌮
What do you think @Liam Cottle ?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 9:03 p.m.
If you look at the complete event, it gives back the semaphore for as many complete events. Maybe we could just give all of them back when it disconnects?
9:03 p.m.
case BLE_GATTS_EVT_HVN_TX_COMPLETE: for(uint8_t i=0; i<evt->evt.gatts_evt.params.hvn_tx_complete.count; i++) xSemaphoreGive(_hvn_sem);
9:04 p.m.
it's using a counting semaphore, which has a total of hvn_qsize available
9:06 p.m.
I think what is happening, is the semaphore is being taken, then it disconnects, and never gives them back. I would have expected the request to take the semaphore would just block forever based on the fact that it had the infinity wait.
9:06 p.m.
I'm just not sure what the reason for the crash is. maybe it's blowing the stack with too many semaphores waiting?
9:07 p.m.
the destructor releases the semaphore, but I'm not sure if we are using a new instance of BLEConnection each time, or if we stay in the same one forever across all connect/disconnect cycles
9:07 p.m.
my hack fix just released the semaphore immediately after, which essentially just disables the whole queue
9:08 p.m.
but I don't really know tbh. probably need some comments from the library author
Liam Cottle
case BLE_GATTS_EVT_HVN_TX_COMPLETE: for(uint8_t i=0; i<evt->evt.gatts_evt.params.hvn_tx_complete.count; i++) xSemaphoreGive(_hvn_sem);
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 9:12 p.m.
something along these lines is what i was thinking.
9:15 p.m.
it might actually be a bug(feature? ) in the softdevice, where the softdevice never sends BLE_GATTS_EVT_HVN_TX_COMPLETE when the disconnect happens due to timeout
Liam Cottle
the destructor releases the semaphore, but I'm not sure if we are using a new instance of BLEConnection each time, or if we stay in the same one forever across all connect/disconnect cycles
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 9:17 p.m.
i am not sure of this either, but i am guessing maybe the destructor never gets called and we are always in the same context. there is only one call to begin after all.
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 9:23 p.m.
https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/blob/4a2d8dd5be9686b6580ed2249cae43972922572f/cores/nRF5/nordic/softdevice/s140_nrf52_7.3.0_API/include/ble_gatts.h#L613
Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino/cores/nRF5/nordic/softdevice/s140_nrf52_7.3....
Adafruit code for the Nordic nRF52 BLE SoC on Arduino - adafruit/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino
9:23 p.m.
My favourite part is the docs say to decrement the queue if it returns success
9:23 p.m.
but, the bluefruit lib decrements it before making the call
9:26 p.m.
either way, I think the semaphores should be released after disconnect
mcarper 2025-11-26 9:30 p.m.
Would you want a not clear check then clear and sleep on semaphore clear? Or just clear and go
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 9:31 p.m.
the soft device tells you if it's busy, in which case the response is not a success code
9:31 p.m.
the way I see it, is the semaphore is meant to be a counter of in flight requests the soft device is handling (edited)
1
9:32 p.m.
if the soft device says it's busy, we haven't queued a new request, so don't touch semaphore (edited)
9:32 p.m.
if it says ok, it's now inflight waiting to tx or whatever
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 9:33 p.m.
i think that's right, this is why getHvnPacket takes a semaphore from the count, that is the queue size that was configured in the softdevice by Bluefruit.begin()
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 9:33 p.m.
but the bluefruit lib does decrement the sempahore before even talking to soft device
9:34 p.m.
yeah, my assumption is the call to acquire the sem is done before talking to soft device, then we get disconnected, so never get a timeout or complete callback
9:34 p.m.
and the semaphore is in whack state forever
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 9:35 p.m.
yeah i can't find anywhere the destructor gets called so the semaphores are never reinstantiated
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 9:35 p.m.
yeah, so same instance forever, which probably means starvation
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 9:35 p.m.
but the ondisconnect does seem to fire, because we get the bluefruit disconnected reason
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 9:36 p.m.
I guess the next test is to add logic to disconnect in bluefruit to release it all
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 9:36 p.m.
i think if we give back the correct number of semaphores in that disconnect handler things might be ok?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-26 9:37 p.m.
I'm currently in between several jobs so can't test this right now
1
9:37 p.m.
but do have a nice setup for reproducing the issue quickly
9:37 p.m.
when I say nice setup, it's a rak4631 with the ble antenna removed
Liam Cottle
when I say nice setup, it's a rak4631 with the ble antenna removed
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 9:39 p.m.
same setup as referenced in the other PR
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-26 11:41 p.m.
ok, i've patched BLEConnection so that on disconnect it should give back _hvn_sem until it's back to hvn_qsize
11:43 p.m.
@Liam Cottle as a side note, did you ever manage to get it to trigger using android? i think you said something about making contactIterator spam constantly and then doing the usual forced timeout disconnect?
che aporeps 🌮
@Liam Cottle as a side note, did you ever manage to get it to trigger using android? i think you said something about making contactIterator spam constantly and then doing the usual forced timeout disconnect?
Liam Cottle 2025-11-27 12:58 a.m.
I never bothered testing android since no one reported the issue. Only tested on the iPhone 15 iOS 18.x (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 12:59 a.m.
No worries, I was just curious more than anything. If it can't be triggered on android I'd love to know what iOS does
1:00 a.m.

Random Citizen
I summon @MrAlders0n , if it works, i'll update btfixv4 too so @recrof can post yet another final solution on telegram
recrof 2025-11-27 2:00 a.m.
no probs. is this yet another semaphore that we patched?
recrof
no probs. is this yet another semaphore that we patched?
Random Citizen 2025-11-27 2:01 a.m.
It's safer, not a nuclear option.
recrof
no probs. is this yet another semaphore that we patched?
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 2:03 a.m.
It's the same one, just instead of not waiting for TX_COMPLETE to release release it, we allow the usual behaviour but then replenish the semaphore on disconnect as well. This way should avoid filling the softdevice's hvn queue, but also reset the semaphore queue on disconnect.
2:03 a.m.
That's the theory anyway
2:09 a.m.
(i also included resetting the indicate semaphore as well, because it looks like the same issue on adafruit github. disconnect reason=0x8 leads to the same crash when sending data using indicate (we use notify))
2:09 a.m.
assuming it works we should push to get it upstreamed to adafruit
1
2:15 a.m.
i checked the nordic softdevice docs, and it seems to say that the way adafruit handles the hvn queue is correct. (edited)
Random Citizen
I summon @MrAlders0n , if it works, i'll update btfixv4 too so @recrof can post yet another final solution on telegram
MrAlders0n 2025-11-27 5:34 a.m.
Yep, I’ll get it flashed today at some point

mcarper 2025-11-27 2:43 p.m.

Cisien
still alive
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 3:04 p.m.
cheers, that's a good sign!
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 3:30 p.m.
i was thinking about the crash itself again last night and because it only seems to affect specific iOS hw/sw combinations it probably is a subtle bug in the softdevice. i'm speculating that iOS throws a packet in the mix where the softdevice isn't expecting it, and that stops the softdevice from emiting any more TX_COMPLETE events which kills the normal flow.
che aporeps 🌮
i was thinking about the crash itself again last night and because it only seems to affect specific iOS hw/sw combinations it probably is a subtle bug in the softdevice. i'm speculating that iOS throws a packet in the mix where the softdevice isn't expecting it, and that stops the softdevice from emiting any more TX_COMPLETE events which kills the normal flow.
Random Citizen 2025-11-27 3:36 p.m.
I read somewhere that iOS transmits some weird heartbeat every 120msec, but I couldn't find that particular document now.
Random Citizen
I read somewhere that iOS transmits some weird heartbeat every 120msec, but I couldn't find that particular document now.
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 3:43 p.m.
i found an ancient thread on nordic devzone that mentions certain android phones which seem to cause the softdevice to stop emitting HVN_TX_COMPLETE. possibly related to the phone sending LL_CONNECTION_UPDATE_REQ when it's not expected or something.
3:44 p.m.
the thread just fizzles out unfortunately because they found a workaround.. which kinda suggests the issue probably remains if you have the right conditions to trigger it
Random Citizen 2025-11-27 3:44 p.m.
workaround it is!
che aporeps 🌮
the thread just fizzles out unfortunately because they found a workaround.. which kinda suggests the issue probably remains if you have the right conditions to trigger it
Random Citizen 2025-11-27 3:45 p.m.
https://community.renesas.com/wireles-connectivity/f/bluetooth-low-energy/25066/how-to-intercpet-ll_connection_update_req-issued-from-master-on-slave-device-running-da14580 ?
3:46 p.m.
I tried catching paramete rupdate requests and answering them in some earlier btfix versions
3:46 p.m.
I saw responses too, but sometimes just NRF response that softdevice already handled it... (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 3:47 p.m.
ble stuff is pretty opaque, hard to tell whats going on to begin with and then softdevice is closed source so nearly impossible to say what the flows are
Random Citizen
I saw responses too, but sometimes just NRF response that softdevice already handled it... (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 3:48 p.m.
yeah i think even catching it maybe couldn't do anything anyway because softdevice has probably already gone "wtf? ok i'm gonna reset the connection" and it loses the hvn queue state entirely, but meanwhile our app is left holding the bag waiting for responses that never come and the hvn queue is full
1
3:49 p.m.
all speculation of course 🫣
Random Citizen 2025-11-27 3:49 p.m.
maybe we should go with the best looking workaround, meshcore has a lot of other stuff to improve, this little sh** took a lot of time already from a lot of us
che aporeps 🌮 2025-11-27 3:51 p.m.
yeah, we should probably get some input from brainy people and hopefully call it a day for the ios disconnect bug. then we can get turbo-ble included
3:52 p.m.
i love turbo-ble, i'm never going back

Random Citizen 2025-11-28 3:05 a.m.
I've replaced bluefruit patch in btfixv4 branch anyone building from that should clear out adafruit framework cache in their environment first. I use Windows so here's how I do it, but it should be in .platformio folder on any OS: Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\.platformio\packages\framework-arduinoadafruitnrf52" -Recurse -Force (edited)
1

Liam Cottle 2025-11-29 6:57 p.m.
Yeah the funky channel sync is the app still syncing channels from previous connection sync request, when you go ahead and start a new one. So it's possible for multiple syncs at once. Should probably patch that
6:58 p.m.

Random Citizen 2025-12-01 4:36 a.m.
While testing i stumbled upon another hard to reproduce bug I updated mc app via obtainium which killed it on install (the app). After that my wio tracker was not advertising for some reason. The node was fine, i saw incoming messages on the screen, but i couldn't connect to it in the app. Of course no matter how many times I force update mc app, connect/disconnect/microwave, it doesn't surface again... (edited)
4:37 a.m.
A simple BT disable/enable in the node resolved it the first time (edited)
4:40 a.m.
Added a simple watchdog that runs every 10 sec: BT enabled? & nothing is connected? & advertising is not running? -> start advertising.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-06 4:43 p.m.
The only fix that is in 1.7.2 is the rename fix as far as I know, and we don't rename files so I can't see it making any difference
4:44 p.m.
Locking out writes when the voltage is low would help though
1
che aporeps 🌮
The only fix that is in 1.7.2 is the rename fix as far as I know, and we don't rename files so I can't see it making any difference
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-06 4:52 p.m.
A rename fix could be big. Right now it looks like we just overwrite the existing file, which might be where our contacts corruption comes from on power loss. Write to .tmp, verify success, then rename to make the update atomic? (edited)
mcarper 2025-12-06 5:14 p.m.
mv Vs fwrite only mv is the better option in theory (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
A rename fix could be big. Right now it looks like we just overwrite the existing file, which might be where our contacts corruption comes from on power loss. Write to .tmp, verify success, then rename to make the update atomic? (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-06 5:18 p.m.
Lose half the space?
5:19 p.m.
Corruption comes primarily from losing consecutive metadata blocks due to power loss on write, and 4k erase pages but 128byte blocks
5:21 p.m.
It wouldn't be that bad to lose half the space, but people wouldn't be happy going to 175 contacts, they want 1000 contacts
5:23 p.m.
I have something in the works but it will be a while yet and will probably need a full fs wipe and be breaking
5:25 p.m.
I do think that write lockout on low battery would solve a most of the corruption problem
Liam Cottle 2025-12-06 7:19 p.m.
Never write. Problem solved
1
1
che aporeps 🌮
Lose half the space?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-06 7:19 p.m.
Agreed, not ideal. I agree that a write lockout, followed by a graceful shutdown into a low power mode on low battery would be the most palatable solution for both repeaters and companions.
2
Liam Cottle
Never write. Problem solved
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-06 7:20 p.m.
That would really bring the MT style joy to MC... every boot you discover everything again. So many notifications. So much dopamine for everyone!
3
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-06 7:34 p.m.
It's a bastard problem

mrlm 2025-12-14 5:03 a.m.
Rak Tag has messed up again four or five times since running dev with the BT fixes and updating the bootloader 0.9.2-OTAFIX1.2-BP1.2. Only response I can get out of it normally is dropping to the drive DFU by double pressing reset and re-flashing everything. This time I managed to catch it and not need to do that. It lost most of its config (name / key / bt pairing) but retained all the contacts and rooms which I believe are on separate storage. (edited)
5:06 a.m.

mrlm 2025-12-14 7:26 a.m.

mrlm

Random Citizen 2025-12-14 7:42 a.m.
tell me if it changes anything about the freezes
mrlm 2025-12-14 7:43 a.m.
Could take a few days, but I have just managed to reproduce the BLE error from here. https://discord.com/channels/1343693475589263471/1437565846435139668/1447269665217708145
7:44 a.m.
The app is now very eager to reconnect so even when my phone is connected the pc is also waiting or trying to do so. Time it right and you see that error.
Random Citizen 2025-12-14 7:44 a.m.
that's not an error, if the BLE link is saturated/slow/on the edge of reception then the internal buffer will be full
7:46 a.m.
or if you disconnect it then the buffer will be full until the disconnection completes / connection times out
mrlm 2025-12-14 7:48 a.m.
It also happens when I put it on the windows sill, just to the right of the desk. For some reason my PC BT has become terrible. So that's the edge of reception.... no its working perfectly on the window sill so its not the PC BT. Perhaps the issue builds up over time. (edited)
mrlm 2025-12-15 7:22 a.m.
The Rak Tag has been fine but I just managed to make the lock up occur with the T1000 and on restart it lost all the config. Both are running the latest LFS patch. The reproduce the fault. Device is connected to vscode monitor and using the PC app over BT. Once the initial sync has occurred I can move the device anywhere the cable will reach and it continues to work. If it move it to the window just before or during the initial connection sync, the vscode monitor fills with the BLE write bytes failed warning. The device then locks up, the vscode monitor stops and it needs resetting to do anything. It seems like the BT signal is a lot weaker from the tiny dongle in the PC and to that location, before the faster BT sync it could cope. If there are lots of contacts this seems more likely to happen and cause the data loss. (edited)
Random Citizen 2025-12-15 7:58 a.m.
no matter how hard I torture my T1000 I cannot reproduce this. not with android, not with PC with an USB dongle
mrlm 2025-12-15 8:02 a.m.
There must be something about my PC or room which makes it worse. BT works really well normally, I can stream LDAC audio across the house and up the garden with no issues. I am not doing this or anything else with BT at the time it happens but it should pick free channels anyway. (edited)
8:05 a.m.
At least it is a very rare occurrence not likely to affect most people but is there anything else I can try to do to capture logs (edited)
Random Citizen 2025-12-15 8:06 a.m.
the whole btfix was for spotty connections, it has a separate queue that fills and signals MC firmware not to send anything when BT is unreliable and acts as a backpressure/guard between the fw and the softdevice (edited)
8:06 a.m.
and on top of that the disappearing config is baffling too
mrlm 2025-12-15 8:10 a.m.
I will try a couple of things. Older firmware before these fixes and getting rid of the little dongle. I can use the Intel AX210 Bluetooth instead.
Random Citizen 2025-12-15 8:11 a.m.
1.11 official is showing the same behaviour?
mrlm 2025-12-15 8:22 a.m.
I don't think so but that's what I am going the check. Similar things have happened before but nowhere near this frequency and always seemed to be related to the file corruption when losing power.
recrof 2025-12-15 10:43 a.m.
it seems that this problem is directly related to flash corruption
mrlm 2025-12-15 12:54 p.m.
Just tried again on 1.11.0 and the lockup doesn't happen it just really slows down, I also don't see the BLE message but I assume that because its part of the new firmware. So it only occurs after multiple difficult Bluetooth connections on this dev firmware and it doesn't always cause a loss of config, I did it just before reverting to 1.11.0 and lost contacts instead so it could just be to do with a particular file being written at the time. (edited)
12:56 p.m.
I know how the flash corruption made it behave before the LFS fixes and I see none of that. It can work normally for weeks without having to restore anything. The T1000 has been used for nearly a year, but the Rak Tag is quite new and sits on the desk normally. I have no reason to believe either of them are damaged or otherwise misbehaving. (edited)
mrlm
Just tried again on 1.11.0 and the lockup doesn't happen it just really slows down, I also don't see the BLE message but I assume that because its part of the new firmware. So it only occurs after multiple difficult Bluetooth connections on this dev firmware and it doesn't always cause a loss of config, I did it just before reverting to 1.11.0 and lost contacts instead so it could just be to do with a particular file being written at the time. (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-15 4:32 p.m.
Yeah losing contacts or config is fs corruption. Even if the device still looks like it works, from that point it's not trustable. There is the odd occasion where the filesystem corruption has caused the filesystem in question to be formatted in which has you've lost some files but it will still work properly but that's the exception not the norm. The question is why are you getting flash corruption if your device isn't running out of power or otherwise restarting.
4:34 p.m.
Are you doing anything else abnormal? Not running 400 contacts?
4:36 p.m.
Flash problems also probably explain why the BLE buffer is filling. Flash calls and Bluetooth calls can't happen at the same time, and if flash calls are taking longer than they should you'll starve the BLE buffer .
mrlm 2025-12-15 4:38 p.m.
That makes sense, what I am doing to make it happen is repeatedly blocking an already weak signal with my hand.
4:38 p.m.
Watching the ble messages and eventually it locks up.
4:39 p.m.
The number of contacts may increase the chances of it happening but I don't have them full now.
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-15 4:40 p.m.
It's very strange, because the latest BLE patch has fixed it for everyone else but for you it seems to have made it worse
mrlm 2025-12-15 4:42 p.m.
If I used my devices with my phone away from home I would probably never see it.
4:43 p.m.
I've been actively looking for it trying to make it happen.
4:43 p.m.
I will now stop doing that, unless it somehow helps.
4:45 p.m.
I have also discovered it has been happening since before these patches. It just doesn't lock up with 1.11.0 and is harder to make happen, probably the tubo ble makes it easier.
mrlm
The Rak Tag has been fine but I just managed to make the lock up occur with the T1000 and on restart it lost all the config. Both are running the latest LFS patch. The reproduce the fault. Device is connected to vscode monitor and using the PC app over BT. Once the initial sync has occurred I can move the device anywhere the cable will reach and it continues to work. If it move it to the window just before or during the initial connection sync, the vscode monitor fills with the BLE write bytes failed warning. The device then locks up, the vscode monitor stops and it needs resetting to do anything. It seems like the BT signal is a lot weaker from the tiny dongle in the PC and to that location, before the faster BT sync it could cope. If there are lots of contacts this seems more likely to happen and cause the data loss. (edited)
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-15 4:46 p.m.
The lockup happens during the initial sync is that right? Not after?
mrlm 2025-12-15 4:46 p.m.
Yes, it has to be really pushing a lot of data at once.
4:46 p.m.
After that i cannot make it happen.
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-15 4:46 p.m.
It can maybe happen after but it's much more likely to happen during initial sync.
4:46 p.m.
Yep ok
4:48 p.m.
@Random Citizen You're the BLE master. I wonder if an extra guard like when the BLE buffer is full and sending the errors in the log, after X errors just can the connection?
mrlm 2025-12-15 4:50 p.m.
I have also seen something similar happen on Xiao nRF in the past but never the ESP boards. (edited)
1
4:56 p.m.
I think the app has also made it worse by reconnecting. It feels like I have had notifications on my phone when I thought it was closed and using the PC app.
che aporeps 🌮
@Random Citizen You're the BLE master. I wonder if an extra guard like when the BLE buffer is full and sending the errors in the log, after X errors just can the connection?
Random Citizen 2025-12-15 6:01 p.m.
I tried that few iterations earlier, but even 1 sec timeout was too long to prevent the lock when semaphores was a thing, so I scrapped that idea. The 2 sec bt guard timeout will kill the connection either way and the internal buffer being full should prevent contact iterator or anything else to try to write to ble. I can kill the connection after the buffer is nearly full but it would make a hell lot more reconnects with devices at the edge of reception. Even closing the microwave for a split second will fill the buffer.
1
Random Citizen
I tried that few iterations earlier, but even 1 sec timeout was too long to prevent the lock when semaphores was a thing, so I scrapped that idea. The 2 sec bt guard timeout will kill the connection either way and the internal buffer being full should prevent contact iterator or anything else to try to write to ble. I can kill the connection after the buffer is nearly full but it would make a hell lot more reconnects with devices at the edge of reception. Even closing the microwave for a split second will fill the buffer.
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-15 6:02 p.m.
You've got more idea than me!
Random Citizen 2025-12-15 6:06 p.m.
One question @mrlm, do you flash erased before flashing dev or factory resetted after flashing? Because flash corruption lingers without full erase, even if you update/re-flash the fw.
mrlm 2025-12-15 6:08 p.m.
Recently I have flash erased with the web one, flashed with vscode and erased with the app before importing config.
6:08 p.m.
Am I being thorough enough?
mrlm
Recently I have flash erased with the web one, flashed with vscode and erased with the app before importing config.
recrof 2025-12-15 6:09 p.m.
you forgot to use gamma rays to really erase the flash
1
mrlm 2025-12-15 6:11 p.m.
Seriously though the reason for that extreme method is because it was the only way I could get the Rak Tag to respond. Just flashing it didnt seem enough. (edited)
6:13 p.m.
I have had experience of other types of things not erasing correctly and issues lingering, so I always think the best erase is a factory reset after new firmware is working. (edited)
mrlm
I have had experience of other types of things not erasing correctly and issues lingering, so I always think the best erase is a factory reset after new firmware is working. (edited)
Random Citizen 2025-12-15 6:39 p.m.
Welcome in the "erase before every update" club
che aporeps 🌮
You've got more idea than me!
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 2:50 a.m.
and you gave me 2 more with a question I'll make some modifications so that checkrcvframe throttle itself immediately if a buffer full scenario is detected and disconnect after 2 seconds. this should cover the edge case when BLE doesn't tear down the connection because it's not a full timeout just a spotty connection but no data can squeeze through and throttle sending for 50msec so it will let the CPU some breathing room. I'll test this today with my own tracker if it behaves normally or not (edited)
1
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 3:31 a.m.
fck I thought I'm finished but down the rabbit hole again
1
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 3:45 a.m.
nah, no crying, it's fun
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 4:19 a.m.
@mrlm can you build from https://github.com/liquidraver/MeshCore/tree/btfixv7 and test it? I added a throttle to retry only every 250msec if queue is full and added slave latency 4. this two should prevent tight loops that maybe causing littlefs to fail (and latency will save some battery power too when connection is idle)
4:23 a.m.
@Liam Cottle do you have some throttle on channel sync in the app? I see exactly 60msec or 360msec gaps when syncing.
Random Citizen
@Liam Cottle do you have some throttle on channel sync in the app? I see exactly 60msec or 360msec gaps when syncing.
Liam Cottle 2025-12-16 4:29 a.m.
flutter uses a single thread, so probably due to the nature of async functions and the fact that it has to wait for response before making next request
4:30 a.m.
but I didn't add any delay logic
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 4:30 a.m.
ah, so no hardcoded magic number
mrlm 2025-12-16 5:35 a.m.
I have spotted something. Every time the app disconnects and reconnects it synchronises all the contacts again sending loads of data. So by putting the device in the spot with poor signal I can see it having issues and going round in a loop. So far it has not crashed though, it is now behaving like it does on 1.11.0 and slowing down. (edited)
5:40 a.m.
Bluetooth pairing has been lost, so it looks like you have solved the issue introduced by these fixes and you are back to the old one. (edited)
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 5:40 a.m.
yeah, every disconnect-reconnect will trigger the full sync, that's by design, because if there is an advert or something coming in between the disconnect-reconnect it needs to be synced or it will sit in the offline queue (you miss "notify" when not connected)
mrlm
Bluetooth pairing has been lost, so it looks like you have solved the issue introduced by these fixes and you are back to the old one. (edited)
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 5:42 a.m.
what do you mean lost?
mrlm 2025-12-16 5:43 a.m.

5:43 a.m.
Or it tries to connect and disconnects.
5:44 a.m.
deleting the pairing and pairing again gets rid of that.
5:44 a.m.
but you cannot do anything
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-16 5:44 a.m.

mrlm 2025-12-16 5:44 a.m.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-16 5:45 a.m.

mrlm 2025-12-16 5:45 a.m.

che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-16 5:46 a.m.
Yeah, that's what factory reset does (edited)
mrlm 2025-12-16 5:46 a.m.

5:47 a.m.
These two devices(t1000/raktag) seem to be most susceptible but that is probably how they are used. (edited)

7:09 a.m.

7:09 a.m.

7:14 a.m.

@che aporeps 🌮 I couldn’t get the idea of "throttle when queue full" & "slave latency" out of my head now. do you think we need those?
1
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-16 8:11 a.m.
I dunno, this is the first I've heard of any problems
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 8:14 a.m.
I played with slave latency earlier because it just means "skip X conn event when nothing to do"
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-16 8:14 a.m.
and it'll be a hard one to nail down if you can't reproduce it
mrlm 2025-12-16 8:14 a.m.
I have definitely seen this before on the phone but it was very rare. Before the the PC app that would have been all I would use unless I wanted USB.
8:14 a.m.
I doubt I could reproduce it so who knows if it is the same. It was only the much faster BT sync that made it obvious (edited)
8:17 a.m.
Could the fact it doesn't happen on ESP32 point to an issue in the softdevice rather than MC code ?
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 8:18 a.m.
everything is pointing to softdevice, always
che aporeps 🌮
I dunno, this is the first I've heard of any problems
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 8:22 a.m.
it's not the problems, it's just two sensible things: 1: if queue is full, we now try to send the frame on every checkrcvframe, hammering on the BT queue and using CPU. Throttle just backs off to "retry every 250msec if queue is full" 2: slave latency is a BT stack stuff that tells the peripheral how many events it can skip listening for if there is nothing to do. 4 means the peripheral wakes up every 5th event (eg 37.5ms intervals in this example instead of every 7.5ms). and only if there is nothing to do, if there are stuff in the queue slave latency won't do anything. - this means battery saving also
8:24 a.m.
I tested slave latency on my wio numerous times in the past weeks, did not make any jitter. The queue throttle is a fresh idea, what if we are on the edge of reception and queue is full, we are hammering the BLE stack, using CPU, and an advert comes in that needs writing to FS...? (edited)
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 8:38 a.m.
bah, I just make a PR and ask for review, what could happen?
che aporeps 🌮 2025-12-16 8:52 a.m.
True that, but it would be good to see it actually solve a problem
che aporeps 🌮
True that, but it would be good to see it actually solve a problem
Random Citizen 2025-12-16 8:56 a.m.
it is not problem solving anymore (thank god), it's optimizing now
1
9:00 a.m.
"slave latency 4" will lower the idle consuption by 80% without noticeable latency average wake interval will be every ~110ms instead of every ~22ms and it's applied only when phone and companion is idle only we are talking about microamps but why not, if we can?
9:01 a.m.
I built my wio from btfixv7, will test it a little before PR-ing

Random Citizen 2025-12-19 6:22 a.m.
there is no better thread to post this, so here it comes: https://github.com/liquidraver/MeshCore/tree/unifyBLE my first shot at unifying BLE interfaces between ESP and nRF. ESP is migrated to nimBLE they have a lof of common things but they can't be fully unified because there are fundamental differences anyone care to try, build nRF or ESP companions from this branch. iOS freezers welcome to test too it has latest dev synced it's raw so expect glitches
1
1

Random Citizen 2025-12-22 10:45 a.m.
I may have found something, Android is an adult, iOS is a toddler that needs everything defined, even if it's default. can you pull and try to build from the branch again? this time the FW "enforces" (it is by default, but iOS ignores it maybe) authentication that should trigger a re-pair

Random Citizen 2025-12-22 11:13 a.m.
I really need to get a used iphone so i won't use you guys as guinea pigs
Random Citizen
I may have found something, Android is an adult, iOS is a toddler that needs everything defined, even if it's default. can you pull and try to build from the branch again? this time the FW "enforces" (it is by default, but iOS ignores it maybe) authentication that should trigger a re-pair
Liam Cottle 2025-12-22 7:33 p.m.
Afaik iOS won't trigger a re-pair for an existing pairing. You have to manually forget the device in iOS settings. In android it will auto clear an existing bond when it detects it's no longer bonded on the other side, and the next connection request will ask for new pin. But on iOS this is not the case. If there is a way to trigger a new pairing for an already paired device on iOS, this would be amazing
1
Random Citizen
I may have found something, Android is an adult, iOS is a toddler that needs everything defined, even if it's default. can you pull and try to build from the branch again? this time the FW "enforces" (it is by default, but iOS ignores it maybe) authentication that should trigger a re-pair

Random Citizen 2025-12-23 5:55 a.m.

recrof 2026-03-26 8:21 a.m.
seems we aren't out of the woods yet.. we don't have lockup problem now, but there is high chance of fs corruption during normal bluetooth companion use even with full battery. I'm using a mac and have my RAK 1W connected outside to mains with extra battery that it's always toped up, but it still gets wrecked after week of use.. contacts are just gone or I'm unable to connect because pairing db is corrupted
8:22 a.m.
I never shutdown the node, so it's not related to low battery or shutdown during writes
recrof
seems we aren't out of the woods yet.. we don't have lockup problem now, but there is high chance of fs corruption during normal bluetooth companion use even with full battery. I'm using a mac and have my RAK 1W connected outside to mains with extra battery that it's always toped up, but it still gets wrecked after week of use.. contacts are just gone or I'm unable to connect because pairing db is corrupted
mrlm 2026-03-26 8:47 a.m.
I had no end of issues on my PC after a certain firmware which turned out to be a combined Intel AX210 WiFi/BT card. It is possible your Mac may be using the same or very similar Broadcom combined WiFi/BT card. The solution was to change back to the cheap tiny BT dongle I thought I was using anyway. I have found that if you disrupt the intial sync it is more likley to disconnect and in some cases cause corruption on the nRF devices. This isnt during the contact or channel sync anymore but during the messages. (edited)
mrlm
I had no end of issues on my PC after a certain firmware which turned out to be a combined Intel AX210 WiFi/BT card. It is possible your Mac may be using the same or very similar Broadcom combined WiFi/BT card. The solution was to change back to the cheap tiny BT dongle I thought I was using anyway. I have found that if you disrupt the intial sync it is more likley to disconnect and in some cases cause corruption on the nRF devices. This isnt during the contact or channel sync anymore but during the messages. (edited)
recrof 2026-03-26 8:48 a.m.
macs don't support bluetooth usb dongles
8:49 a.m.
and yeah, it's broadcom: BCM_4388C2
mrlm 2026-03-26 8:50 a.m.
Is it an old enough mac you can test it in bootcamp with another OS?
8:50 a.m.
A VM would be pointless unless you can passthrough a USB device.
mrlm

recrof 2026-03-26 8:51 a.m.
nope. it's brand new apple macbook pro m5
mrlm 2026-03-26 8:53 a.m.
Not used Arm Macs but VMware Fusion might let you, I think they made it free as well. (edited)
